steps.instruction: Label culture tubes with the name of the construct (or shortened nickname), your initials, and the date. If you are using nicknames, you must create a table in your ELN detailing the designated nickname for each construct. See example below: Nickname: KZK10_A Full name: DHY213_KZK10_02302030_A
Sample Preparation

To the erlenmeyer flask, add the desired amount of culture media. Use the following formula to calculate total culture media where x = number of cultures: (5 mL * x) + 2 mL = total culture media required

Obtain the appropriate glycerol stock from storage at -80 °C on DRY ICE . Use an inoculation loop to gently scrape the top of the frozen glycerol stock. Once you observe a small amount of opaque liquid on the tip of the loop, dip into the corresponding culture tube and swirl for 00:00:10 seconds. Discard the loop into the sharps container. Repeat for each sample. Be extremely careful when handling glycerol stocks. Minimize exposure (always close lid when not in use). Always keep on DRY ICE. Only touch the top third (furthest from the tip) of the inoculation loop. This will help prevent contamination of the culture.
Sample Preparation

Using a loop, gently scrape a single colony from the plate, ensuring not to dig into the solid agar . Visually observe the white colony on the tip of the loop. Dip into the corresponding culture tube and swirl for 00:00:10 seconds. Discard the loop into the sharps container. Repeat for each sample. Only touch the top third (furthest from the tip) of the inoculation loop. This will help prevent contamination of the culture.
Decontamination
Wipe benches and pipettes with DNAaway, RNase Aaway. Afterwards wipe benches and pipettes with 70% ethanol.
Incubation
Incubate the cultures for the recommended duration at the appropriate shaking speed for the sample.
Place the culture tubes in the incubator tube rack at the appropriate temperature for the sample. Ensure the lids are moveable (not locked down) so that air can flow into the tubes.
Rinse erlenmeyer flask with water in the sink, hang on the drying rack to be washed.
Incubation
Place the culture tubes in the incubator tube rack at the appropriate temperature for the sample. Ensure the lids are moveable (not locked down) so that air can flow into the tubes.
Incubate the cultures for the recommended duration at the appropriate shaking speed for the sample.
Decontamination
Rinse erlenmeyer flask with water in the sink, hang on the drying rack.
Wipe benches and pipettes with DNAaway, RNase Aaway. Afterwards wipe benches and pipettes with 70% ethanol.
Label
To erlenmeyer flask, add the antibiotic at a 1:1000 ratio. For example : For 17 mL of media, add 17 µL of ampicillin.
Swirl flask for approximately 00:00:10 seconds to fully mix.
Using a serological pipette, aliquot 5 mL culture media + antibiotic to each labeled culture tube.
Wipe benches and pipettes with DNAaway, RNase Aaway. Afterwards wipe benches and pipettes with 70% ethanol.
Decontamination
Setup
Light the bunsen burner. All work should be performed close to the bunsen burner.
Return agar plates to the proper storage location.
Return glycerol stocks to storage at -80 °C immediately after use.
Storage
Storage
